risk_description: Distributed transaction inconsistency across Project, Payment, and Notification services during financial workflows (e.g., Invoice Paid -> Project Active -> Email Sent). Failure in one leg could leave system in split-brain state.
root_cause: Event-driven microservices architecture lacks inherent ACID properties across service boundaries; relying on eventual consistency without robust orchestration.
mitigation_strategy: Implement Saga pattern with compensation transactions for all financial workflows. Ensure idempotency keys are enforced on all payment Webhooks.
contingency_plan: Develop automated reconciliation scripts to run nightly comparing internal Ledger with Stripe/Wise reports; manual override tools for Admins (US-065).
monitoring_trigger: Detection of 'Orphaned' transactions or DLQ message count > 0 for financial queues.

risk_description: AI hallucination or failure in PII sanitization logic (AWS Comprehend) resulting in sensitive data leakage into the Project Brief or Vendor matching embeddings.
root_cause: Probabilistic nature of LLMs and NLP services; variability in SOW document formats uploaded by clients.
mitigation_strategy: Strict enforcement of 'Human-in-the-Loop' workflow (US-034/US-035). Aggressive PII confidence thresholds. Do not persist raw SOWs in accessible storage.
contingency_plan: Emergency purge protocol to scrub vector database and recreate embeddings; Legal notification workflow.
monitoring_trigger: Admin flagging 'Missed PII' during the SOW review step more than 5% of the time.

risk_description: Stripe Connect or Wise platform account verification delays or regulatory blocks for the master merchant account, halting all financial processing.
root_cause: Dependency on third-party KYC/KYB processes and international financial regulations outside team control.
mitigation_strategy: Initiate platform account verification immediately (Day 1). Implement modular payment adapter pattern to allow switching providers if necessary.
contingency_plan: Manual invoicing workflow as temporary fallback; alternative payment gateway selection (e.g., PayPal/Adyen).
monitoring_trigger: Stripe Connect account status remaining 'Pending' for > 5 business days.

risk_description: Performance degradation in vector similarity search (`pgvector`) as vendor and project datasets scale, impacting the 'Recommended Vendors' load time.
root_cause: Full scan behavior of basic vector search if proper indexing (HNSW/IVFFlat) is not tuned for the specific dimension size of OpenAI embeddings.
mitigation_strategy: Implement HNSW indexing on `pgvector` columns. Cache recommendation results. Offload calculation to background job upon Brief Approval.
contingency_plan: Vertical scaling of RDS instance; migration to dedicated vector DB (Pinecone/Weaviate) if Postgres limits are hit.

risk_description: Broken Access Control (BAC) where Vendors/Clients manipulate API calls to view data belonging to other tenants/projects.
root_cause: Complex RBAC requirements (internal vs external roles) combined with sequential numeric IDs or insufficient ownership checks in NestJS guards.
mitigation_strategy: Use UUIDs everywhere. Implement rigorous integration tests for negative authorization scenarios (checking ownership, not just role).
contingency_plan: Immediate revocation of tokens; Incident Response Plan activation; Forensics on Audit Logs.
monitoring_trigger: Spike in 403 Forbidden errors in API Gateway logs from authenticated users.

risk_description: Cost explosion and performance degradation of OpenSearch due to high volume of immutable audit logs required by compliance (every write/view).
root_cause: Requirement to log every CRUD action and view event (US-086) creates massive write volume.
mitigation_strategy: Implement Index State Management (ISM) policies in OpenSearch to move older logs to cold storage (UltraWarm/S3) after 30 days.
contingency_plan: Sample read-access logs instead of 100% capture (if compliance permits); Increase cluster storage limits.
monitoring_trigger: OpenSearch cluster free storage dropping below 20%.

risk_description: Testing bottleneck due to complexity of mocking external dependencies (AWS SES, Stripe, Wise, OpenAI) in CI pipelines.
root_cause: High number of third-party integrations required for core user stories to pass E2E tests.
mitigation_strategy: Invest heavily in LocalStack and specialized mocks (Stripe CLI, MailHog) within the Docker Compose dev environment. Establish contract tests.
contingency_plan: Reduce E2E test coverage in favor of Integration tests; Manual QA for third-party flows before release.
monitoring_trigger: CI pipeline duration exceeding 20 minutes or flakiness rate > 10%.
